meaning: n.代理,代用品,代理人vt.使代理,使代替
examples: She saw him as a sort of surrogate father.
Arms control should not be made into a surrogate for peace.
He found a psychotherapist near him to supervise his work with a surrogate, and flew the surrogate there from Los Angeles.
One of them became a surrogate parent after his own father abandoned the family.
Surrogate arrangements are usually set up through agencies, although private arrangements can be made.
